Technology.detail: SpaceX's newest and biggest Starship will have to wait at least another day before launching on a test flight from Texas due to last minute problems.
Technology.headline: SpaceX tries to launch a bigger version of Starship but hits a series of last-minute problems
Apple registers new 'gen AI' web domain as Siri gears up for its biggest upgrade in years
Technology.detail: Apple is said to be testing a new Extensions system inside the App Store that could allow users to plug in third-party AI assistants like Claude or Gemini with Siri. This feature could be similar to how Siri has been routing the more difficult conversations to ChatGPT over the last couple of years.

Nvidia Corp CEO Jensen Huang arrived in Taiwan ahead of upcoming AI and technology events, saying he plans to meet with clients and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Chairman C.C. Wei during his visit. Huang also mentioned that higher memory prices could significantly affect consumer electronics prices and that it is a challenge for consumers.
Technology.headline: Nvidia's Jensen Huang arrives in Taiwan
Robot turns heads on main street as tech divide narrows
Technology.detail: A humanoid robot strolling past shoppers on the main street of a Sunshine Coast town has sparked mixed reactions, highlighting the narrowing gap between emerging technology and everyday life. The robot, Unitree G1, is owned by the University of the Sunshine Coast and is used to help students explore how humanoid systems can operate in real-world environments.
Meta joins Snap, TikTok, and YouTube in settling a social media addiction case, though the specific terms of the settlement remain undisclosed. Many other similar cases are still pending.
Technology.headline: Meta Joins Snap, TikTok, YouTube in Settling Social Media Addiction Case

Science.detail: A team led by Yuki Kawabata found that crabs' sideways walking originated from an ancestor that roamed the Earth about 200 million years ago and may have helped them diversify and succeed due to its advantage in escaping predators. The sideways walking evolution occurred during the Early Jurassic period from a forward-walking ancestor.
Science.headline: Origin of crab walk dates back some 200 million years
NASA stunned as strange solar radio burst lasts 19 days
Science.detail: NASA scientists were surprised when a radio signal from the Sun persisted for 19 days, far exceeding the typical duration of such bursts. The signal was traced to a massive magnetic structure on the Sun known as a helmet streamer and may have been sustained by three coronal mass ejections.

Science.headline: Scientists Warn That Current Vitamin B12 Guidelines May Be Putting Your Brain at Risk
Science.detail: Researchers at UC San Francisco found that older adults with “normal” but lower levels of active B12 showed signs of slower thinking, delayed visual processing, and more damage to the brain's white matter. Getting enough vitamin B12 to meet current health guidelines may not actually be enough to protect the aging brain.

Scientists have created an AI-powered system that can scan and map an entire mouse body in extraordinary detail, uncovering a surprising new effect of obesity: damage to facial sensory nerves. Beyond disrupting metabolism, obesity appears to damage facial sensory.
Science.headline: New AI Body Map Reveals Obesity's Hidden Attack on Facial Nerves

Science.detail: A newly named crocodile species from Ethiopia likely lived alongside Lucy's species and dominated the region. This giant crocodile hunted human ancestors.
Science.headline: Scientists Discover Terrifying Giant Crocodile That Hunted Human Ancestors

Lifestyle.headline: International Tea Day 2026: How to brew the perfect cup of Darjeeling tea?
Lifestyle.detail: We find out how to brew the perfect cup of Darjeeling tea in Flurys Kolkata.
A neurologist warns that heatwaves can increase the risk of stroke, as dehydration can thicken the blood.
Lifestyle.headline: Neurologist warns against stroke risk in summer: How heatwaves impact the brain

Lifestyle.detail: Gen-Z women are increasingly influencing and reshaping the trends and styles associated with homeownership.
Lifestyle.headline: Gen-Z women are changing the look of homeownership

Lifestyle.detail: Choupette, the world's richest cat, has a lifestyle that includes luxuries and pampering fit for royalty, drawing comparisons to Cleopatra.
Lifestyle.headline: $200 million kitty: Meet Choupette, the world's richest cat, whose lifestyle would make Cleopatra envious

Lifestyle.detail: Horchata, a cold and creamy beverage, is gaining popularity and is expected to appear on more menus as an alternative to matcha lattes.
Lifestyle.headline: Move over matcha lattes: horchata is cold, creamy and coming to a menu near you

Sports.detail: Iran were expected to fly from Turkiye to Arizona to continue World Cup 2026 preparations but will switch to Mexico.
Sports.headline: Iran move World Cup base from US to Mexico with FIFA approval

Sports.detail: Barcelona defeated Lyon with a score of 4-0 to win the Women's Champions League.
Sports.headline: Barcelona beat Lyon 4-0 to win Women's Champions League

Sports.detail: The King of Morocco has pardoned jailed Senegal football fans who were imprisoned following the AFCON final.
Sports.headline: Morocco king pardons jailed Senegal football fans from AFCON final
Hull beat Boro in world's most lucrative game to reach Premier League
Sports.detail: Hull City defeated Middlesbrough in what is considered the world's most lucrative game, securing their promotion to the Premier League.

Sports.headline: Cowherd Reacts to Stafford's Extension: 'Win A Super Bowl Now And Next Year'
Sports.detail: Colin Cowherd discusses Stafford's contract extension and the pressure to win a Super Bowl in the near future.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Human rights compliance
Opinion/Editorial.detail: In 2009, early in his first term of office, former president Ma Ying-jeou of the Chinese Nationalist Party (KMT) announced the ratification of the UN's International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights and the International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights, showing Taiwan's dedication to human rights.
Taiwan is positioning itself internationally as a technological powerhouse and a mature democracy, and for Poland, this story carries a surprisingly familiar resonance.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Taiwanese culture reaches Europe

Opinion/Editorial.detail: A new law on advance medical directives in Hong Kong will help ensure dignity at the end of life.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: SCMP Editorial | Law on advance medical directives will help ensure dignity at end of life

Opinion/Editorial.detail: The indie hit 'Dear You' in Hong Kong demonstrates the power of storytelling, proving that impactful films don't always require large budgets.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: SCMP Editorial | Indie hit Dear You shows the power of storytelling over big budgets

Opinion/Editorial.detail: ThinkCareBelieve releases a report on Week 70 of the Trump 2.0 Administration, covering events since January 2025 and providing details and links to primary sources.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: ThinkCareBelieve: Week 70 of the Trump 2.0 Administration

Finance & Economy.headline: Economy keeps on stable growth path: High-tech manufacturing, AI lead momentum, policy support to step up
Finance & Economy.detail: China's economy shows resilience with strong expansion in high-tech manufacturing and AI, while experts call for stronger domestic demand and targeted measures amid geopolitical tensions and energy costs. China is expected to invest over 5 trillion yuan during the 15th Five-Year Plan period (2026-30) to build a safer, greener power grid.

Britain's retail sales fell 1.3 percent in April, the steepest monthly decline since May 2025, due to weak consumer confidence and higher fuel costs amid the Middle East conflict. Analysts suggest the figures highlight market vulnerability and a subdued outlook for the rest of 2026, as the Middle East conflict is expected to further weigh on consumer spending.
Finance & Economy.headline: Economic Watch: Britain's retail sales suffer sharpest monthly fall in April over past year

Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. economic growth remains steady with first-quarter GDP expanding at a 2.0% annualized rate, above the long-term trend. While consumer spending is slowing, business investment in digital equipment and software is surging, driven by AI and related infrastructure, though it cannot fully offset shifts in consumer spending.
Finance & Economy.headline: AARON TAYLOR: The U.S. economy is still growing
Stable China-US economic, trade relations bring certainty to the world
Finance & Economy.detail: China and the United States are working towards stable economic and trade relations through consultations and preliminary agreements, reducing uncertainty and promoting stability in bilateral trade. The two countries are establishing trade and investment councils to manage concerns, reflecting a pragmatic approach to win-win cooperation and benefiting businesses and consumers.

Finance & Economy.headline: Progress made by US and Iran on peace deal
Finance & Economy.detail: Progress has been reported in peace negotiations between the US and Iran. The discussions aim to de-escalate tensions and potentially lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, which is crucial for global oil supplies.

World.detail: A suspect was killed after opening fire on a Secret Service checkpoint outside the White House, and a bystander was also wounded. Officers returned fire after the suspect pulled a weapon from a bag.
World.headline: Suspected gunman dead after exchanging fire with Secret Service near White House
Trump says US-Iran peace deal is 'largely negotiated'
World.detail: According to Trump, a peace deal with Iran has been largely negotiated, with the Strait of Hormuz expected to reopen as part of the agreement. He also stated that he will meet with US negotiators to finalize Iran's ceasefire proposal.
A coal mine explosion in China has resulted in the deaths of 90 people, marking it as the deadliest such incident in recent years. The incident is under investigation.
World.headline: Deadliest coal mine explosion in China in years kills 90 people

World.detail: Cristian Mungiu's film 'Fjord' has been awarded the Palme d'Or at the Cannes Film Festival. The film has been receiving high praise from critics.
World.headline: 'Fjord' by Romania's Mungiu wins top Palme d'Or film award at Cannes

World.detail: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has stated that the Ebola outbreak is spreading rapidly, with a significant increase in suspected cases. Uganda has confirmed 3 new Ebola cases, as 10 more countries are 'at risk' of virus.
World.headline: Ebola outbreak 'spreading rapidly' as suspected cases rise, WHO head says

Crime & Justice.headline: 2 wounded in shooting near White House, sources say
Crime & Justice.detail: Two people were wounded in a shooting near the White House; the suspect and a possible bystander were taken to a hospital, with the suspect in critical condition. Sources say the suspect opened fire on a Secret Service security booth, and Secret Service agents returned fire.

Crime & Justice.headline: NBI expects more ICC arrest warrants soon
Crime & Justice.detail: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) in the Philippines anticipates that the International Criminal Court (ICC) may issue additional arrest warrants soon. NBI Director Melvin Matibag stated they have received information suggesting more warrants may be issued in connection to ongoing ICC proceedings regarding alleged crimes against humanity.

Crime & Justice.headline: Bukit Aman to investigate those linked to discovery of prohibited items at Ipoh HQ lock-up
Crime & Justice.detail: Bukit Aman's integrity and standards compliance department (JIPS) will handle the investigations into police officers and personnel believed to be linked to the discovery of prohibited items at Ipoh police headquarters lock-up. A surprise inspection uncovered various prohibited items, including drugs, mobile phones and sharp weapons, inside a cell at the police station on May 12.

A woman is accused of opening fire outside a courthouse in Raleigh, North Carolina, and shooting two lawyers. The shooting occurred after a civil case turned criminal, and both lawyers were hospitalized.
Crime & Justice.headline: Woman accused of shooting 2 lawyers
Trump's justice department scrubs its website of news releases about January 6 defendants
Crime & Justice.detail: The Department of Justice acknowledged the removal of information about criminal cases related to the 2021 US Capitol rioters from its website. It is noted that Trump dismissed a $10 billion suit against the IRS and created a $1.7 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund.

Education.headline: 'STEM education must evolve'
Education.detail: With Malaysia attracting more high-tech investments, science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) education must move beyond technical knowledge and focus on building adaptable, future-ready students who can apply technology across disciplines. The country's first AI faculty at Universiti Teknologi Malaysia is an example of efforts to build local expertise in STEM.

Education.headline: West Bengal Class 12 board examination results declared
Education.detail: The West Bengal Class 12 board examination results were declared with an overall pass percentage above 91%. Other educational news includes CBI arresting more accused in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak investigation and Oracle reportedly revoking campus placement offers made to students from IITs and NITs after recent layoffs.

Education.headline: U.S. Department of Education Approves Louisiana's Returning Education to the States Waiver
Education.detail: The U.S. Department of Education approved Louisiana's Returning Education to the States Waiver, empowering State education officials with greater discretion over their federal education dollars. Also, ED announced it has approved Florida and Illinois' Ed-Flex applications, marking a record high number of 18 states now utilizing Ed-Flex authority.

Education.headline: AI literacy is becoming an essential skill
Education.detail: Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk Chang Lih Kang stated that artificial intelligence (AI) literacy is becoming an essential skill not only for science and technology students, but across all fields, as workplaces increasingly rely on digital tools and automation. He added that STEM education today must also encourage students to think about how emerging technologies can be integrated into their own fields and professions.

The Global Education Monitoring Report 2026 finds that with the 2030 deadline for achieving Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 4 only a few years away, progress in ensuring access to education falls short. Since 2000, enrollment has increased significantly across all levels of education, but the out-of-school population has been rising for seven consecutive years.
Education.headline: Global Education Monitoring Report 2026: Progress in ensuring access to education falls short of Sustainable Development Goal

Entertainment.detail: Director Cristian Mungiu won the Palme d'Or award for the film 'Fjord' at the 79th Cannes Film Festival, while 'Minotaur' took the Grand Prix.
Entertainment.headline: Cannes 2026 WINNERS: Cristian Mungiu's Fjord wins Palme d'Or, Minotaur takes Grand Prix

Entertainment.detail: Prince William remained secretive when questioned about attending Taylor Swift's wedding.
Entertainment.headline: Prince William plays coy when asked if he scored a seat at Taylor Swift's wedding
How Lady Gaga BLOCKED Brooke Lynn Hytes From 'The Devil Wears Prada' Cast (Exclusive)
Entertainment.detail: Entertainment Tonight provides exclusive details on how Lady Gaga may have influenced the casting of 'The Devil Wears Prada'.

Entertainment.headline: Kyle Busch's cause of death revealed by family
Entertainment.detail: Kyle Busch, who won more races in NASCAR's top three series than anyone in history, died suddenly on Thursday; the cause of death has been revealed by his family.

Entertainment.headline: Battlestar Galactica bombshell Tricia Helfer makes bold career move by joining adult social platform at 52
Entertainment.detail: Tricia Helfer, known for her role in Battlestar Galactica, has joined an adult social platform at the age of 52, marking a bold career move.

Politics.headline: Gunman killed after opening fire on Secret Service checkpoint outside White House, officials say
Politics.detail: A gunman was killed after opening fire on a U.S. Secret Service checkpoint outside the White House Saturday evening; Secret Service officers returned fire, hitting the suspect, who was taken to a hospital, where he died, and President Trump was at the White House during the incident, but was not impacted.

Politics.headline: Trump to meet with US negotiators to decide on Iran's ceasefire proposal
Politics.detail: President Trump is scheduled to meet with U.S. negotiators to discuss Iran's ceasefire proposal amidst ongoing efforts to de-escalate tensions in the Middle East.

Politics.headline: Thousands march in Madrid demanding Spanish PM quit over graft allegations
Politics.detail: Thousands of protesters marched in Madrid, calling for the Spanish Prime Minister to resign due to allegations of corruption.

Politics.headline: Trump briefed on shooting outside White House, official says
Politics.detail: President Trump has been briefed on the shooting that occurred outside the White House, according to a White House official; the Secret Service confirmed that Mr. Trump was at the White House when the incident occurred and has been busy this weekend working to negotiate a peace deal to end the war with Iran.
The United States has announced murder charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro.
Politics.headline: US announces murder charges against former Cuban president Raúl Castro – video

Zimbabwe.headline: Eight Roman Catholic parishioners killed in commuter omnibus crash
Zimbabwe.detail: A commuter omnibus crash resulted in the death of eight Roman Catholic parishioners. The accident details are still emerging.

Zimbabwe.headline: Pair arrested for vandalising ZESA transformer, unlawful possession of copper windings
Zimbabwe.detail: Two individuals were apprehended for vandalizing a ZESA transformer and possessing copper windings illegally. Authorities are investigating the extent of the damage and the suspects' motives.
A man was caught attempting to smuggle drugs and other substances valued at US$12,000 across the border. The incident highlights ongoing efforts to combat cross-border smuggling.
Zimbabwe.headline: Man busted while attempting to smuggle drugs and substances worth US$12,000 across border

Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa stated that public officials who demand bribes from investors are enemies of development. He emphasized the importance of ethical conduct to foster economic growth.
Zimbabwe.headline: 'Public officials demanding bribes from investors are enemies of development': Mnangagwa
Body of murdered young man found tied to pole in Waterfalls
Zimbabwe.detail: The body of a young man was discovered tied to a pole in Waterfalls. Police are investigating the circumstances surrounding the murder.

Health.detail: Kyle Busch died after severe pneumonia progressed into sepsis, resulting in rapid and overwhelming complications, according to a statement released by his family.
Health.headline: Kyle Busch died after severe pneumonia led to sepsis, his family says

Health.detail: A recent study has linked higher caffeine intake to greater depression severity.
Health.headline: Higher Caffeine Intake Tied to Greater Depression Severity
Gut-lung microbe shifts may explain clozapine's severe bowel and lung side effects
Health.detail: Research suggests that shifts in gut-lung microbes may be the reason for clozapine's severe bowel and lung side effects.

Health.headline: FDA proposes ban on bulk compounding of semaglutide and tirzepatide
Health.detail: The FDA has proposed a ban on the bulk compounding of semaglutide and tirzepatide.

Health.headline: Deadly measles outbreak in Bangladesh fuels concern over risks for U.S.
Health.detail: A deadly measles outbreak in Bangladesh is raising concerns about potential risks in the U.S.
